methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $35M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.5B - $7.5B.

private.name: Flock Safety

private.name: Shield AI

data.narrative:
Rebellion Defense is a Series G defense and security company that has raised $150M to date. The valuation analysis is anchored to a peer set of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies spanning private and late-stage funding, supplemented by reference to 2 public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and a broader universe of 8 private frontier-defense peers including Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Anduril Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised ($150M) as a proxy for base value and applying a 1.25x multiplier typical of Series G advancement, implies a valuation of $44M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 comps—Flock Safety ($7.5B), Shield AI ($5.6B), Skydio ($2.2B), and Destinus ($1.5B)—yields a median valuation of $3.9B, with a range of $1.5B to $7.5B.

The triangulated valuation range is $37M (low) to $4.5B (high), with a median of $2.0B. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around which peer cohort is most comparable. Stage-progression implies a substantially lower valuation, suggesting either that Rebellion Defense remains pre-revenue or early-traction relative to later-stage peers, or that the $150M raised represents a conservative proxy. Key uncertainties include revenue traction, customer concentration, and the company's competitive position within the sector.
